original_query: select  count(*) from (     select distinct c_last_name, c_first_name, d_date     from store_sales, date_dim, customer           where store_sales.ss_sold_date_sk = date_dim.d_date_sk       and store_sales.ss_customer_sk = customer.c_customer_sk       and d_month_seq between 1185 and 1185 + 11       and c_birth_month in (2, 3, 10, 12)       and ss_list_price between 28 and 87       and ss_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 80 AND 100   intersect     select distinct c_last_name, c_first_name, d_date     from catalog_sales, date_dim, customer           where catalog_sales.cs_sold_date_sk = date_dim.d_date_sk       and catalog_sales.cs_bill_customer_sk = customer.c_customer_sk       and d_month_seq between 1185 and 1185 + 11       and c_birth_month in (2, 3, 10, 12)       and cs_list_price between 28 and 87       and cs_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 80 AND 100   intersect     select distinct c_last_name, c_first_name, d_date     from web_sales, date_dim, customer           where web_sales.ws_sold_date_sk = date_dim.d_date_sk       and web_sales.ws_bill_customer_sk = customer.c_customer_sk       and d_month_seq between 1185 and 1185 + 11       and c_birth_month in (2, 3, 10, 12)       and ws_list_price between 28 and 87       and ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 80 AND 100 ) hot_cust limit 100;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_date AS (
    SELECT d_date_sk, d_date
    FROM date_dim
    WHERE d_month_seq BETWEEN 1185 AND 1196
), filtered_customer AS (
    SELECT c_customer_sk, c_last_name, c_first_name
    FROM customer
    WHERE c_birth_month IN (2, 3, 10, 12)
)
SELECT COUNT(*)
FROM (
    SELECT c_last_name, c_first_name, d_date
    FROM (
        SELECT DISTINCT 
            fc.c_last_name, 
            fc.c_first_name, 
            fd.d_date,
            1 AS sales_source
        FROM store_sales ss
        JOIN filtered_date fd ON ss.ss_sold_date_sk = fd.d_date_sk
        JOIN filtered_customer fc ON ss.ss_customer_sk = fc.c_customer_sk
        WHERE ss.ss_list_price BETWEEN 28 AND 87
          AND ss.ss_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 80 AND 100
        UNION ALL
        SELECT DISTINCT 
            fc.c_last_name, 
            fc.c_first_name, 
            fd.d_date,
            2 AS sales_source
        FROM catalog_sales cs
        JOIN filtered_date fd ON cs.cs_sold_date_sk = fd.d_date_sk
        JOIN filtered_customer fc ON cs.cs_bill_customer_sk = fc.c_customer_sk
        WHERE cs.cs_list_price BETWEEN 28 AND 87
          AND cs.cs_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 80 AND 100
        UNION ALL
        SELECT DISTINCT 
            fc.c_last_name, 
            fc.c_first_name, 
            fd.d_date,
            3 AS sales_source
        FROM web_sales ws
        JOIN filtered_date fd ON ws.ws_sold_date_sk = fd.d_date_sk
        JOIN filtered_customer fc ON ws.ws_bill_customer_sk = fc.c_customer_sk
        WHERE ws.ws_list_price BETWEEN 28 AND 87
          AND ws.ws_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 80 AND 100
    ) combined_sales
    GROUP BY c_last_name, c_first_name, d_date
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT sales_source) = 3
) hot_cust
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Use Common Table Expressions (CTEs) to pre-filter date_dim and customer tables, reducing redundant filtering operations across the three sales queries.
Replace multiple INTERSECT operations with a UNION ALL approach combined with GROUP BY and HAVING, which can be more efficient for finding records that exist in all three sales channels.
Add a sales_source identifier to track which sales channel each record comes from, allowing the use of COUNT(DISTINCT sales_source) = 3 to identify customers present in all three channels.
